label: Renewable Energy Consumption from Other Sources

sections.about: This metric captures renewable energy consumed through sources other than electricity and fuel under BRSR Principle 6, Essential Indicator 1. It is usually a residual category and is often zero where renewable energy is fully classified as electricity or fuel. A higher value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: Only Tata reported a non-zero value in FY2023 at 20,000 GJ on a consolidated basis; all peers reported zero. In FY2024 and FY2025, all four companies reported zero, meaning renewable energy was captured under electricity or fuel rather than other-source categories. The category does not appear to be a strategic differentiator for any company in FY2025.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2025, Tata ranks 1st, tied with JSW, SAIL and Jindal at zero. There is no percentage gap because all companies reported the same value. Tata’s ranking remains rank 1 due to ties, but its absolute value worsened from 20,000 GJ in FY2023 to zero in FY2024 and stayed zero in FY2025.
sections.targets: No targets identified for this specific metric. Broader renewable energy targets exist for Tata, SAIL and Jindal, but none are stated for “other renewable sources” specifically.
sections.comparabilityNotes: This category is residual. A zero value is not necessarily poor performance if renewable energy is reported under electricity or fuel.
